$schema: The six seam primitives. This set is CLOSED. Every seam, classic or frontier, is exactly one of these. The agent era added no seventh primitive; it exploded edge/node count for P1 and P2 and inverted blast radius.

primitives.name: Data->Control
primitives.definition: Input parsed as instruction. The boundary between data and code is treated as enforced when it is not.
primitives.agent_era_note: Exploded. The LLM is a parser with no grammar: any text is potential control. There is no parameterized query for English.

primitives.name: Identity->Authority
primitives.definition: Position or credential wielded by a party that should not hold it. The system authorizes the position, not the intent behind it.
primitives.agent_era_note: Inverted blast radius. The deputy used to be a human with judgment and human pace; now it is an agent with the human's scope, zero judgment, machine speed.

primitives.name: Provenance
primitives.definition: Output trusted for where it came from, when origin is forgeable. The receiver checks the label, not the thing.
primitives.agent_era_note: Retrieval and memory create new forgeable-origin surfaces: write-side open, read-side privileged, no boundary check between.

primitives.name: Context-Inheritance
primitives.definition: Downstream inherits parent trust without re-validation. Composition unions authority but not scrutiny.
primitives.agent_era_note: Runtime composition of skills/plugins/memory each inherit host authority: as exposed as the least-vetted component, as trusting as the most-vetted.

primitives.name: Format-Boundary
primitives.definition: Meaning shifts crossing a parser. Two components disagree about where a thing begins and ends.
primitives.agent_era_note: New transports (MCP stdio/SSE/streamable-HTTP, tool-call framing) reintroduce boundary desync between broker and backend.

primitives.name: Time/State
primitives.definition: True at check, false at use. The validated fact and the acted-upon fact are separated in time.
primitives.agent_era_note: Long-lived agent state, memory replay, and detector feedback loops widen the check-to-use gap across whole sessions.
